title: Driver-Team Strategy Prior Proxies
summary: Public F1 pit datasets emphasize driver/team behavior, average stop patterns, stint length, and pit frequency as predictive strategy variables.
rationale: Public F1 pit datasets emphasize driver/team behavior, average stop patterns, stint length, and pit frequency as predictive strategy variables. The current data lacks Team, but Driver/Race/Year combinations can still encode conservative vs aggressive stop tendencies.
implementation_hint: Build smoothed OOF priors: driver_pit_rate, driver_compound_pit_age_median, race_pit_lap_density, year_compound_pit_rate, stint_count_prior, and interaction encodings for Driver x Compound and Race x Stint. Feed these alongside raw categorical columns into CatBoost and LightGBM.
expected_effect: Adds strategic tendency information beyond instantaneous tyre and pace variables.
risk: High-cardinality priors can memorize random splits; require race/year grouped validation to estimate leaderboard robustness.
